assessments.status_rationale: Today’s items focus on U.S./Israeli strikes on Iran and market impacts, but do not report EASA issuing a CZIB/SIB advising European operators to avoid or mitigate risk in Iran/Iraq airspace.
There is travel coverage about the conflict’s impact on flights, but no clear evidence that Delta, United, or American suspended all scheduled passenger flights to DXB/DOH/AUH for 7 consecutive days.
News highlights the Strait of Hormuz as a chokepoint, but does not state that the Lloyd’s JWC added it (or adjacent Persian Gulf waters) to the Listed Areas or expanded boundaries via circular.
Articles note oil price jumps after attacks, but there is no BLS CPI release evidence here showing the CPI-U gasoline index up at least +10.0% month-over-month.
No FOMC post-meeting statement is quoted or referenced in today’s news as explicitly mentioning “oil prices” or “energy prices” as an inflation factor.

Today’s political coverage discusses the Iran war and U.S. politics, but provides no evidence of a recorded House roll-call vote on a War Powers Resolution (or analogous privileged measure) regarding U.S. military action against Iran.
No item indicates a state enacted or filed in federal court a new congressional map explicitly described by major outlets as responding to a Supreme Court Voting Rights Act ruling.
No evidence in today’s bullets of a UN Security Council emergency meeting (with official UN record/readout) specifically on Afghanistan–Pakistan cross-border hostilities.

CNBC references “Operation Epic Fury,” but the provided bullet does not clarify whether it is a U.S. DoD/CENTCOM-announced named maritime security operation/task force aimed at protecting commercial shipping in/near the Strait of Hormuz (as opposed to a broader strike campaign name).

Coverage references Berkshire leadership communications (shareholder letter highlights), but does not provide a quarterly 10-Q/10-K figure confirming ≥$3.0B in share repurchases in a filing covering results through Q3 2026.
